description: Retry-After RFC 850 legacy date-form accept cases. These rows exercise the capability gain that landed when parse_retry_after migrated to httpdate::parse_http_date: the previous hand-rolled IMF-fixdate parser rejected this form, while RFC 7231 section 7.1.1.1 admits it for backwards compatibility with the obsolete RFC 850 wire syntax. The two-digit year is interpreted per the documented sliding-window rule (years 70-99 map to 19xx; years 00-69 map to 20xx).

cases.description: Canonical RFC 7231 RFC 850 example — same instant as the IMF-fixdate canonical example, distinct wire syntax.
Two-digit year 99 maps to 1999 under the documented sliding-window rule.
End of 1973 — exercises an early-decade two-digit-year branch where 73 maps to 1973.
End of 1970 + one second — close to the epoch boundary inside the RFC 850 accept range.
Year 38 maps to 2038 under the sliding-window rule and the resulting timestamp is exactly the i32 signed boundary commonly known as the Y2038 instant.
